frameworks.name: Bayesian causal inference
frameworks.one_line: audiovisual integration weighted by sensory uncertainty for spatial localization

frameworks.name: optimal Bayesian learner with exponential discounting
frameworks.one_line: uncertainty estimates combine past and current signals via exponential decay

frameworks.name: instantaneous independent uncertainty estimation
frameworks.one_line: leading view that brain computes uncertainty per-stimulus without history

frameworks.name: perceptual inference
frameworks.one_line: how the brain combines noisy signals to form reliable percepts

core_question: Does the brain estimate sensory uncertainty instantaneously per stimulus, or does it integrate past experience?
key_assumptions: observers weight multisensory inputs by their estimated uncertainty
auditory localization is biased by spatially disparate visual signals
Bayesian observer model captures multisensory integration
manipulations: visual noise level changed dynamically over time
continuous vs intermittent (jump) changes in visual noise
spatial disparity between auditory and visual signals
key_findings: observers integrate audiovisual inputs weighted by uncertainty combining past and current signals
behavior is consistent with an optimal Bayesian learner approximated by exponential discounting
results deviate from models using only current-stimulus uncertainty
interpretations: the brain exploits temporal dynamics of the world to estimate sensory uncertainty
uncertainty estimation combines past experience with new incoming signals
